title: Continue.dev Cheat Sheet
description: Essential guide for engineers using Continue.dev for coding assistance.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative code solutions or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4o model with streaming output.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time code suggestions as you type.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the AI to reason through problems methodically.
Limits the response length to manage cost and focus.
commands.scenario: When you need concise code snippets or explanations.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When integrating into systems with strict response time requirements.

commands.description: Instructs the AI to generate code compatible with Python 3.8.
commands.scenario: When working on projects that require specific language versions.

commands.description: Reduces repetition in the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating varied code examples or documentation.

commands.description: Requests a plain language explanation of code.
commands.scenario: When onboarding new team members or learning new codebases.

commands.description: Sets the logging level to debug for detailed output.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting integration issues with the API.

commands.description: Encourages the AI to introduce new topics or ideas.
commands.scenario: When exploring innovative solutions or new features.

commands.command: "Summarize the key points of this code."
commands.description: Asks the AI to provide a summary of the code's functionality.
commands.scenario: When documenting code or preparing reports.

commands.description: Retries the API request up to 3 times on failure.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in automated systems.

commands.description: Instructs the AI to optimize code for performance.
commands.scenario: When enhancing existing code for better performance.

commands.description: Uses local embeddings to perform semantic search across your entire codebase for context-aware answers.
Configure custom context providers (like docs, terminal, or git) to feed specific metadata into the LLM prompt.
Quickly adds selected code blocks to the Chat sidebar context without manual copying.
Triggers 'Inline Edit' mode to apply code transformations directly within the active file editor.

commands.command: System Prompt: "You are an expert in [Framework]. Only use [Version] syntax."
commands.description: Advanced prompt pattern to constrain the LLM to specific library versions and prevent deprecated API suggestions.
